rationale: The citing paper uses Hedgcock and Rao (2009) in its theoretical development to support a key claim that decision makers often seek choice strategies that avoid detailed attribute trade-offs. That trade-off-avoidance idea is then embedded in the paper's hierarchical process account and linked to its process evidence (memory for attribute differences and attribute-importance prompts). The citation is not method reuse or a simple literature-list mention; it serves as conceptual grounding for the mechanism the authors build on.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Hedgcock and Rao (2009) as conceptual support for the trade-off-avoidance mechanism in context-dependent choice. It incorporates that mechanism into a broader hierarchical framework to explain when attraction and extremeness-aversion effects emerge.
